name: Mustard Sauce
recipeIngredient: Enough Artificial Sweetener to substitute for 1 tablespoon Sugar
recipeInstructions:
1. Add enough Artificial Sweetener to blend with 1 tablespoon of sugar.
2. Beat egg yolks with dry mustard in the top of a double boiler until blended.
3. Gradually add vinegar, beating after each addition.
4. Cook over simmering water, stirring constantly until thick and smooth.
5. Gradually add milk, beating in with a fork or wire whip.
6. Continue cooking for 5 more minutes over simmering water.
7. Remove from heat and let cool for 10 minutes.
8. Add remaining ingredients (prepared mustard, lemon juice) and blend well.
9. Serve with Ham, Pork, or Canadian-style bacon.
Makes 8 servings of 1 tablespoon each.
Nutritive Values Per Serving: CHO 1 gm; PRO 1 gm; FAT 1 gm; Calories 18 Sodium 28 mg
Food Exchange Per Serving: Up to 1 Tablespoon may be considered "Free". If you use 3 Tablespoons count as 1 Fat Exchange.
Low-sodium Diets: This recipe is suitable, but should be used with fresh pork, not ham or Canadian style bacon.
